regions: Europe, the Middle East and Africa (EMEA)

dependencies: Dependence on operational systems and employees of third parties, market participants and service providers
Reliance on custodians, vendors, clearing and payment systems, CCPs and securities exchanges
Interconnectivity with clients, customers and external parties
risks: Reliance on external data and operational systems
Failure of third-party service providers could disrupt operations
Interruption of service from third-party providers
Concentration risk with clearing houses and exchanges
Cyber attacks on vendors and market infrastructure
Security breaches affecting clients or customers
Widespread interconnectivity among financial institutions increasing systemic risk
Dependency on technology infrastructure and systems

regulatory_risks: Volcker Rule restrictions on proprietary trading
FDIC special assessments and deposit insurance requirements
Higher taxes, assessments, levies or other governmental charges
Conduct of business and control requirements
Ring fencing requirements separating core banking from markets activities
Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) and Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR) requirements
GSIB surcharge requirements and potential amendments
Position limits and reporting rules for derivatives
Resolution planning and living will requirements
Climate-related regulatory requirements and disclosure obligations
Tax law changes and international tax regulations
Privacy and data protection laws (U.S. and international)
Total Loss-Absorbing Capacity (TLAC) and eligible long-term debt (LTD) requirements
Extensive and constantly-evolving regulation and supervision in financial services
Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review (CCAR) and stress testing
Compliance with complex and evolving U.S. and non-U.S. laws, rules and regulations
Requirements to maintain minimum amounts of capital or liquidity in locally-based subsidiaries
Foreign banking regulations (PRA, FCA in UK; ECB in EU)
Limitations on late payment, overdraft and interchange fees
Consumer protection laws and CFPB oversight
LIBOR transition and benchmark rate regulations
Securities and derivatives regulations (SEC, CFTC, FINRA)
Bank Secrecy Act and anti-money laundering requirements
Basel III capital requirements and potential changes
Basel III capital requirements and potential changes (U.S. Basel III Finalization proposal)
Federal Reserve regulatory oversight and restrictions
Differences in financial services regulation across jurisdictions creating competitive disadvantages
Data protection, privacy, cybersecurity and AI regulations (DORA, EU AI Act)
Conflicts in laws across different jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Challenges from inconsistent and conflicting sanctions regimes across jurisdictions
Litigation in Russia related to payments blocked by sanctions
Conflicts between sanctions laws in different jurisdictions
Sanctions on Russia, Iran, and other countries
Sanctions targeting entities in countries involved in terrorism, hostilities, embezzlement or human rights violations
Potential seizure of Firm assets in Russia
Russian court judgments disregarding sanctions laws
Russia sanctions enforcement and asset seizures
Potential curtailment of operations in countries aligned against the U.S. in hostilities
Targeted cyber attacks due to geopolitical tensions
Risk of new sanctions or regulatory requirements due to geopolitical conflicts
Blocking of assets under international sanctions laws
Economic sanctions programs administered by OFAC, EU and UK authorities
